info.title: llama-chat-web REST API
info.description: HTTP REST API for the llama-chat-web backend server (default port 18080). Handles local model inference, conversation management, HuggingFace Hub downloads, MCP server management, remote providers, tool execution, and system monitoring.

ChatMessage.description: A single message in a conversation.

compacted.description: True if this message has been compacted (summarized).

sequence_order.description: DB sequence order for precise truncation.

conversation_id.description: Existing conversation ID to continue. If omitted a new conversation is created.

image_data.description: Base64-encoded image data URIs (e.g. 'data:image/png;base64,...') for vision models.

auto_continue.description: When true the user message is not logged — the model resumes an interrupted generation.

ChatResponse.description: Immediate response from POST /api/chat. Real content arrives via WebSocket.

TokenData.description: A single SSE token event emitted by /api/chat/stream.

content.description: Raw conversation text (empty for remote provider conversations).

ToolTags.description: Tool call XML tag delimiters auto-detected from the model.

model_path.description: Absolute path to the GGUF model file.

gpu_layers.description: Number of layers to offload to GPU. Omit for auto.

mmproj_path.description: Absolute path to the mmproj companion GGUF for vision models.

cache_type_k.description: KV cache quantization type for K (e.g. 'f16', 'q8_0', 'turbo2').

cache_type_v.description: KV cache quantization type for V.

provider_api_keys.description: JSON blob of provider API key configurations.

thinking_mode.description: Enable thinking/reasoning mode. null = use model default.

destination.description: Absolute path to the local destination directory.

oneOf.description: HTTP transport — connects to a running MCP server over HTTP.

tool_name.description: Tool name: read_file, write_file, edit_file, undo_edit, insert_text, search_files, find_files, list_directory, bash, shell, command, web_fetch, browser_navigate, browser_search, browser_eval, browser_get_text, browser_close, or any desktop tool.

get.description: Returns a simple OK response to confirm the server is alive.
Returns application version, platform, architecture, and compiled feature flags.
Returns a machine-readable list of all API endpoints.
Returns current system resource utilisation. Values are sampled and cached for 500ms.
Returns all alive background processes started by tool execution (e.g. servers launched by execute_command with background=true).

post.description: Terminates a background process by PID (process tree kill).
Sends an abort signal to stop any in-progress desktop automation tool calls.
Accepts batches of frontend log entries and writes them to timestamped log files. Maximum 200 entries per request.

get.description: Returns the most recent app-level errors stored in the database.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of errors to return.

post.description: Stores a frontend or runtime error in the database for later review.

delete.description: Deletes all stored app error records.

post.description: Submits a user message to the local model for generation. Returns immediately with the conversation_id; real streaming content arrives via the WebSocket at /ws/conversation/watch/{id}.

200.description: Generation started. Content streams via WebSocket.

503.description: No model loaded or worker unavailable.

post.summary: Send a chat message with SSE streaming (local model)
post.description: Submits a user message and streams tokens back using Server-Sent Events. Each event is a JSON object matching the TokenData schema. The stream ends with 'data: [DONE]'.
Sends a cancellation signal to the worker to stop the active generation.

get.description: WebSocket upgrade endpoint. After upgrade the server streams generation tokens to the client. Requires 'Upgrade: websocket' headers.

get.summary: WebSocket: watch conversation for new messages
get.description: WebSocket upgrade endpoint. The server pushes new messages and generation events for the specified conversation as they arrive.
WebSocket upgrade endpoint. The server sends an initial model status message and then keeps the connection alive with pings. Used by the frontend to detect server crashes and reload state.
Returns the currently saved sampler and application configuration.

post.description: Saves a new sampler/app configuration. Validates temperature (0-2) and top_p (0-1) before saving.

400.description: Validation failed (invalid temperature, top_p, or context_size).

get.description: Returns provider API key configuration with values masked (first 4 + last 4 characters shown).

post.description: Stores or updates the API key and optional base URL for a provider. Send empty api_key and no base_url to remove a provider.

get.description: Returns the currently selected provider (local or a remote provider ID) and model.

post.description: Switches the active provider. Use 'local' to switch back to the local GGUF model.

get.description: Returns all conversations sorted by creation time (newest first). Supports optional text search.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Filter conversations by title, display name, or ID containing this term (case-insensitive).

post.description: Creates a new conversation record with the given title.

delete.description: Deletes multiple conversations by ID in a single request.

ids.description: List of conversation IDs to delete.

delete.description: Deletes a conversation and its associated persisted screenshots. ID must start with 'chat_'.

patch.description: Updates the title of an existing conversation.

post.summary: Truncate conversation at a sequence order
post.description: Deletes all messages with sequence_order >= from_sequence. Used for edit and regenerate functionality.

from_sequence.description: All messages at or after this sequence_order will be deleted.

post.description: Manually triggers conversation compaction (summarisation) via the worker process.

patch.description: Replaces the stored compaction summary for a conversation.

delete.description: Removes the compaction summary, reverting the conversation to its full uncompacted state.

get.description: Returns in-memory debug events (stalls, compaction, tool calls, etc.) for a conversation.
Returns persisted metrics log entries (timing, token counts) for a conversation.
Estimates token usage per role (system, user, assistant, tool responses) for the conversation using character-count heuristics.
Returns the sampler configuration for a specific conversation (falls back to global config if no override is set).

post.description: Saves a sampler configuration override that applies only to this conversation.

get.description: Returns the full message history for a conversation. Tool call/response pairs are reconstructed into the streamed widget format.
Exports a conversation as Markdown or JSON. Returns a downloadable file.

post.summary: Queue a message for injection during active generation
post.description: Queues a user message to be injected into the conversation while the model is actively generating (interrupt-and-continue).

get.description: Returns the loaded model status including load state, generation status, context size, and token overhead.
Reads and returns GGUF metadata for a model file: architecture, context length, quantization, recommended parameters, chat template, and detected mmproj files.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: URL-encoded absolute path to the GGUF file.

400.description: Missing or invalid path, or path is a directory.

post.description: Instructs the worker process to load the specified model. Persists context/cache parameters to global config.
Force-kills the worker process to reclaim all CUDA memory and automatically restarts it. Equivalent to hard-unload.

post.summary: Force-kill worker to reclaim all VRAM
post.description: Immediately kills the worker process, reclaiming all GPU and system memory. A fresh worker is automatically started.

get.description: Returns a list of recently loaded model file paths.

post.summary: Add a path to model history
post.description: Manually adds a model path to the recent history list.

get.description: Returns all compute backends (CUDA, Vulkan, CPU, etc.) with device info and VRAM. Also indicates whether an NVIDIA GPU was hardware-detected and whether CUDA is loaded.

post.summary: Download and install GPU backend DLLs
post.description: Downloads the CUDA backend DLL from the GitHub release and places it in the app directory. Returns an SSE stream with progress events.

200.description: SSE progress stream. Events: {type:'progress', bytes, total, percent} or {type:'done', path, bytes} or {type:'error', message}.

get.description: Returns all provider backends (local, OpenAI-compatible, CLI tools like Claude Code, Codex) with their availability status.
Returns only non-CLI providers that have been configured with API keys.
Checks availability of CLI-based providers (Claude Code, Codex) by detecting their executables.

get.summary: Fetch available models from a provider
get.description: Calls the provider's /v1/models endpoint and returns the model list. Requires the provider to have an API key configured.

400.description: No API key or base URL configured.

post.description: Runs a full agentic generation loop with the specified provider and returns the complete response when done.

post.summary: Generate with a remote provider (SSE streaming)
post.description: Runs a full agentic generation loop with the specified provider, streaming tokens via SSE. Events have type 'token', 'status', or 'done'.

200.description: SSE stream. Token events: {type:'token',token:string}. Done event: {type:'done',provider,session_id,stop_reason,cost_usd,duration_ms,input_tokens,output_tokens,model,conversation_id}.

get.description: Returns all built-in native tools with their JSON schemas (used to populate model system prompt).

post.description: Executes a named tool with provided arguments. Supports: read_file, write_file, edit_file, undo_edit, insert_text, search_files, find_files, list_directory, bash/shell/command, web_fetch, browser_navigate, browser_search, browser_eval, browser_get_text, browser_close, and all desktop automation tools.

get.summary: Fetch a web page as plain text
get.description: Fetches the URL, strips HTML, and returns the text content. Useful for quick curl-style access without a model context.

post.description: Accepts raw file bytes in the body and returns extracted plain text. Supports PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, EPUB, ODT, RTF, ZIP, CSV, EML, and plain text files (with encoding detection).

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Filename with extension (used for type detection, e.g. 'report.pdf').

get.description: Returns all configured MCP (Model Context Protocol) server definitions.

post.summary: Add or update an MCP server
post.description: Saves an MCP server configuration. If the ID already exists it is updated.

400.description: Validation failed (missing id, name, or transport config).

delete.description: Deletes the MCP server configuration with the given ID.

post.summary: Enable or disable an MCP server
post.description: Toggles the enabled state of an MCP server without removing its configuration.
Instructs the worker to reconnect to all enabled MCP servers and rediscover available tools.

get.description: Returns all tools currently discovered from connected MCP servers, grouped by server.
Lists files and directories at the given path. Defaults to /app/models. Directories and GGUF files are shown; results are sorted with directories first.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: URL-encoded absolute directory path to browse.

post.description: Shows the operating system's native directory picker dialog and returns the selected path. Returns null path if the user cancels.
Shows the operating system's native file picker dialog filtered to .gguf files. Returns null path if the user cancels.
Uploads a GGUF model file to /app/models. Maximum 100MB. Filename is taken from the Content-Disposition header or filename query parameter.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Destination filename (used if Content-Disposition header is absent).

get.summary: Search HuggingFace Hub for GGUF models
get.description: Proxies the HuggingFace API to search for GGUF models, avoiding CORS restrictions from the browser.

get.summary: List GGUF files in a HuggingFace repo
get.description: Fetches the file tree of a HuggingFace model repository and returns only the GGUF files with their sizes.

post.summary: Download a model from HuggingFace Hub
post.description: Downloads a GGUF file from HuggingFace to the local filesystem. Supports resume (HTTP Range requests + .part files). Returns an SSE stream with progress events. If the file already exists, returns a done event immediately.

200.description: SSE download progress stream. Events: {type:'progress',bytes,total,speed_kbps} or {type:'done',path,bytes} or {type:'error',message}.

get.description: Returns all hub download records stored in the database (both pending and completed).

delete.description: Deletes a download record from the database and removes the associated .part and final files from disk.

post.description: Checks all download records against the filesystem, removes records for files that no longer exist, and returns the clean list.

get.description: Serves a screenshot image stored by the take_screenshot tool. Supports .jpg, .jpeg, and .png.
